summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to undocumented third-party host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) reads the installer's OS username via os.userInfo() and parses ~/.gitconfig to extract user.name and user.email, then sends all three values as query parameters to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext via https.get. The code branches on the installer's OS username: when it equals the author's own username ('xmarcgusmano') it targets localhost, but for every other installer it sends to the third-party host. The destination domain does not match the publisher's declared domain and is not mentioned in the README, so the installer has no opportunity to opt in or out. A companion postinstall script (dist/scripts/postinstall.js) unlinks preinstall.js, postinstall.js, prepublish.js and postpublish.js immediately after running — destroying on-disk evidence of what executed. The combination of author-vs-everyone-else branching, exfiltration of installer identity data to a non-publisher host at install time, and self-deleting lifecycle scripts is an unambiguous supply-chain attack pattern against the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
